The new preview build looks different from the one everyone tried in June — here’s how to use the head start

6 min read
Just now

--

If you played with Midjourney’s V8.2 preview a few weeks ago and decided “eh, not that different” — go look again. It changed.
Midjourney pushed another refresh to the V8.2 preview at the start of July, and the aesthetic has quietly shifted. The build you judged in June isn’t the build that’s about to ship. And every signal from the community right now points the same direction: the full public release is close. Like, days-away close.
Which means we’re in the most valuable window there is — the gap between “it’s testable” and “everyone has it.” Here’s what actually changed, why it matters if you make or sell AI art, and exactly how to use the head start.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Quick catch-up
If you’ve lost track of Midjourney’s version soup, here’s the clean version:

• V8.1 is the current default — the speed-and-resolution update. Fast, high-res, what you’re using right now unless you change something.

• V8.2 is the next one, and it’s still in preview. It’s not about speed…
